core_behavior.note: Model B specifies reject-only with non-destructive validation and fail-all vs fail-fast modes; Models A and C are silent on reject vs sanitize-and-continue, which is an unspecified design choice in the requirement.

inputs.note: Model B enumerates an extensive list of input sources and rule types including HTTP contexts, CLI, and inter-service payloads; Models A and C describe generic field types without specifying input channels or rule taxonomies.

outputs.note: Model B specifies HTTP 422 with structured JSON error payload, CLI stderr with exit code 2, and dead-letter queue behavior; Models A and C describe generic error messages and validation results without channel-specific output formats.

assumptions.note: Model B assumes a declarative externally-defined schema cached at startup; Model A assumes predefined rules including sanitization; Model C assumes synchronous operation and updatable rules without altering core logic.

failure_modes.note: Model B specifies channel-specific failure behaviors including dead-letter queues and exit code 2; Model A specifies logging and blocking processing; Model C specifies parsing failure and configuration error responses.

edge_cases.note: Model B includes async uniqueness checks, sensitive field redaction, and fail-fast configuration; Model A covers null/empty/boundary/special characters; Model C covers maximum input size and special characters.

security_constraints.note: Model B focuses on sensitive field redaction in error responses and content safety (null bytes, control characters); Models A and C focus on SQL injection and XSS sanitization; Model C additionally requires error messages not expose system internals.

performance_constraints.note: Model A specifies under 1 second per validation; Model C specifies under 100 milliseconds per input; Model B provides no performance constraints.

summary: ASM (Assumption Divergence) was the most frequently assigned category, appearing in 4 of the 8 fields, reflecting that the underspecified requirement led models to independently introduce different design choices across inputs, core behavior, outputs, assumptions, and edge cases.
